description_long: This sculpture features a realistic, life-sized human figure emerging from or crawling out of a rectangular stone or tiled structure. The figure is depicted in a dynamic pose, with one arm extended forward and the other bent, as if reaching or pulling itself out of the opening. The stone structure has a tiled surface with two embedded plaques or images on its top face, adding visual interest and context. The human figure is rendered in a brownish material, contrasting with the lighter, beige stone of the main structure. The overall composition creates a dramatic and somewhat surreal effect, suggesting themes of emergence or escape. The craftsmanship is detailed, especially in the anatomy and posture of the human figure.
description: A dramatic sculpture of a human figure crawling out of a tiled stone structure, with detailed anatomy and embedded plaques on top.
description_view_0: Front view showing the human figure's face and outstretched arm emerging from the stone structure's opening.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the figure's body and the tiled surface with plaques on top.
description_view_2: Rear view showing mostly the back of the stone structure, with only the figure's arm and head visible above.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the figure's back and the structure's tiled surface, with plaques visible.
attribute_subject_matter: human figure emerging from a stone structure
attribute_location_context: The sculpture appears suited for an outdoor urban or park setting, possibly in a plaza or near a public walkway.
